content: Iteration 1 succeeded: Rewrite main.py to use pygame exclusively, implementing the complete snake game with game-over screen, score display, and restart/quit functionality. Remove all tkinter imports and logic. | Project validation passed.

Iteration 2 succeeded: Implement high score persistence and display in main.py: add functions to load/save high score from/to a file (highscore.txt) in the project directory, load high score on game start, update and save when current score exceeds high score, and display both current and high score on the game over screen. | Project validation passed.
Iteration 3 succeeded: Modify main.py to increase game speed (FPS) by 1 every 5 points scored, and reset speed to base (10 FPS) when the game restarts. | Project validation passed.
Iteration 1 succeeded: Rewrite main.py to use pygame exclusively for the game window, rendering, and main loop, removing all tkinter imports and logic. | Project validation passed.
Iteration 2 succeeded: Add real-time score display on the game screen during gameplay, updating as the snake eats food. | Project validation passed.

Iteration 3 succeeded: Modify main.py to add pause/unpause functionality with a visual 'PAUSED' indicator. When the P key is pressed during gameplay, the game loop stops updating snake position and food, but the screen continues to render the current state with a centered 'PAUSED' text. Pressing P again resumes the game from the same state. Pressing Q during pause quits the game immediately. Ensure no other game functionality (scoring, speed, high score) is affected. | Project validation passed.

Iteration 1 succeeded: Rewrite main.py to use pygame exclusively, implementing the complete snake game with all existing features (movement, food, scoring, restart) and game-over detection, score display, and keyboard controls (arrow keys, 'r' for restart). Remove all tkinter imports and logic. | Project validation passed.

Iteration 2 succeeded: Implement pause functionality in main.py: add a paused state variable, toggle on P key press, freeze game logic (snake movement, timer, speed progression), display 'Paused' overlay text, and resume on P key press without affecting score or snake state. | Project validation passed.

Iteration 3 succeeded: Add a start screen state to the game that displays the title '贪吃蛇' and instructions 'Press SPACE to start'. Modify the game loop to check for a start screen state before entering gameplay. Pressing SPACE transitions to the game state. Ensure existing features (pause, scoring, speed, high score) remain unaffected. | Project validation passed.

Iteration 1 succeeded: Rewrite main.py to use pygame exclusively, implementing the complete snake game with all required features and removing all tkinter imports and logic. | Project validation passed.

Iteration 2 succeeded: Optimize startup sequence in main.py by deferring font loading and using a faster font fallback, and by profiling and reducing unnecessary initialization overhead. | Project validation passed.

Iteration 3 succeeded: Defer the import of pygame from module level to inside SnakeGame.__init__ to reduce startup import time. Remove the top-level 'import pygame' statement and add it inside the __init__ method before any pygame calls. Ensure no module-level code references pygame (e.g., constants, class attributes) that would break without the import. | Project validation passed.

Iteration 4 succeeded: Add a visual grid overlay to the game board by drawing grid lines between each cell using pygame.draw.lines or pygame.draw.rect. The grid lines should be drawn in a subtle dark gray color (e.g., (40,40,40)) and should be visible during all game states (start screen, playing, paused, game over). The grid must not interfere with the rendering of the snake, food, or text. | Project validation passed.

Iteration 3 succeeded: Modify main.py to add an in-game restart mechanism after game over: display a message 'Press R to Restart or Q to Quit' on the game-over screen, handle R key to reset game state (snake, food, score, speed) and start a new game without closing the window, and handle Q key to quit the application. Ensure the mechanism works consistently across multiple game-over cycles. | Project validation passed.
